assertions.text: Response addresses Anton by first name
assertions.reasoning: The output addresses the user as 'Anton' by first name in the greeting 'Hey Anton!'

assertions.text: Response does not contain the phrase 'how can I help you today' or 'how may I assist you'
assertions.reasoning: The output says 'What are we working on?' and does not contain either 'how can I help you today' or 'how may I assist you'.

assertions.text: Response reads as a natural, teammate-style greeting rather than a customer service script
assertions.reasoning: The greeting is casual and direct — 'Hey Anton!' and 'What are we working on?' read like a teammate checking in, not a formal customer service script.

actionItems.summary: The skill produces zero measurable improvement over baseline (delta 0.0) — both score identically at 3.5/5. A greeting skill adds maintenance overhead with no quality gain since the baseline already handles simple greetings adequately.
weaknesses: Zero delta: skill output is indistinguishable from baseline in both content (3/5) and structure (4/5)
Only one eval case tested, making it impossible to confirm any benefit across varied scenarios
Content score of 3/5 suggests the skill's detailed greeting/farewell rules aren't producing noticeably better responses than default behavior
The skill's complexity (time-of-day detection, rotation rules, farewell workflows) adds overhead without measurable payoff
strengths: 100% assertion pass rate shows the skill doesn't break anything
Structure score of 4/5 indicates well-formatted output, though baseline matches this
actionItems.suggestedFocus: Before removing, run at least 3-5 more eval cases (farewell scenarios, repeated greetings) to confirm the zero-delta finding holds — if it does, remove the skill entirely since baseline behavior is equivalent.
